# Description

<!--
Summarize the change, why it is needed, and which SDK areas or repository paths are affected.
-->

## Type of change

- [ ] `feat`: New SDK feature or capability
- [ ] `fix`: Bug fix or behavioral correction
- [ ] `docs`: Documentation or example update
- [ ] `refactor`: Internal restructuring without intended behavior change
- [ ] `test`: Test update
- [ ] `build`: Dependency, packaging, or tooling change
- [ ] `ci`: Workflow or automation change
- [ ] `security`: Security or trust-boundary hardening
- [ ] `BREAKING CHANGE`: Intentional incompatible change
- [ ] `chore`: Other maintenance work

## Scope

- SDK area or API surface:
- Paths touched:
- Backward-compatibility impact:

## Related issues / discussions / resources

<!--
Examples:
- Closes #123
- Related to #456
- Follow-up to <design note, docs page, or issue>
-->

## Pre-merge checklist

- [ ] I have read and followed [`CONTRIBUTING.md`](./.github/CONTRIBUTING.md) and [`CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md`](./.github/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md).
- [ ] This pull request is scoped to one logical change.
- [ ] I ran `make format`.
- [ ] I ran `make lint`.
- [ ] I ran `make test`.
- [ ] I updated examples and docs where needed.
- [ ] I considered generated-code boundaries, security, and backward compatibility.
- [ ] I documented breaking changes clearly if this is a breaking change.
